title: Chunked Transfer Coding, Extensions, Trailers, and Decoding · Go Semantics Case: Zero Chunk Completion
summary: A decoder parses chunk sizes as decimal, trusts extensions, misses overflow, forgets the zero chunk, merges forbidden trailers into headers, and leaves bytes misaligned. Planned artifact: Add and verify the zero chunk completion increment in incremental chunked decoder and trailer policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Treat the last zero-sized chunk as the transition to trailer parsing rather than immediate message completion.
Read exactly each chunk-data length plus its terminating CRLF across arbitrary fragmentation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Zero Chunk Completion · Theory Zero Chunk Completion
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 5927 in a signed webhook receiver behind an HTTP/2 to HTTP/1.1 gateway. Build deterministic Go evidence to bound chunk accumulation and require trailer validation after the zero chunk; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then reject a protocol transition while optimistic bytes are buffered and prevent desynchronization. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat the last zero-sized chunk as the transition to trailer parsing rather than immediate message completion.
